Object.Name: AppMessaging
Object.Description1: This LwM2M Object provides a bi-directional messaging facility for applications. Distinct resources are defined for Client to Server messaging and Server to Client messaging.

Item.Name: Message Priority
Item.Description: Indicates the message priority: 0: Immediate 1: BestEffort 2: Latest 3-100: Reserved for future use. 101-254: Proprietary mode.

Item.Name: Message Type
Item.Description: Classifies the payload carried by the app message. Applies to both Client Message (Resource 1) and Server Message (Resource 0). Not all types apply in both directions. 0: CONFIG – App-facing desired or effective configuration state. 1: COMMAND – Action request to perform work now or soon. 2: COMMAND_RESULT – Result of a COMMAND execution, distinct from transport-level response acceptance. 3: STATUS – Current state, lifecycle, readiness, mode, or policy/quota state. 4: EVENT – Operationally meaningful occurrence worth recording or routing. 5: ALARM – Actionable, high-severity, security, safety, or policy condition requiring prompt attention or special handling. 6: APP_DATA – Supplemental application-specific data that intentionally does not fit CONFIG, COMMAND, STATUS, EVENT, ALARM, COMMAND_RESULT, or TELEMETRY. 7: TELEMETRY – Measurement-oriented numeric or structured observations, usually time-series, snapshot, interval, or resource metrics. 8-100: Reserved for future use. 101-254: Proprietary use.

Item.Name: Message TTL
Item.Description: Time-to-live for the current app message in seconds. A value of 0 indicates no message-specific TTL and allows the platform or EMS to apply default policy. Expired messages SHALL NOT be delivered as current app messages, though expiration may be recorded through event or log mechanisms where appropriate.

Item.Name: Content Type
Item.Description: Media type for the app message payload, such as application/json, application/cbor, application/octet-stream, or a GEISA or vendor-specific media type. This allows routing and interpretation without assuming all message payloads are opaque. application/octet-stream may be used for opaque payloads.

Item.Name: AppID
Item.Description: A unique local-scope identifier for distinguishing edge applications within a specific deployment environment.
